extra.reasoning: The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b never be jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose guard is a 12-way conjunction that INCLUDES immunosuppressed. rec_drug_b is set true ONLY by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), whose guard is just immunosuppressed. Since the rec_drug_a conjunction implies immunosuppressed=true, any input that makes rec_drug_a true necessarily also satisfies the rec_drug_b guard. There is no higher-priority rule overriding either output (each output has a single true-setting rule and no false-setting rule besides the default, which only applies when an output is left unset). Setting all 12 conjunction variables true fires both rules, yielding rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. pregnancy is irrelevant (only affects rec_drug_c) and set false for a minimal witness.
